exact_matches.text: Which of the following could best be described as a civil rights or non-discrimination law?
Which of the following could best be described as a civil rights or non-discrimination law?
Which of the following could best be described as a civil rights or non-discrimination law?
Which of the following could best be described as a civil rights or non-discrimination law?
Which of the following could best be described as a multi-national standard?
Which of the following is an example of a procurement law?
Which of the following could best be described as a domain-specific law or regulation?
The *Référentiel Général d'Accessibilité pour les Administrations* (RGAA) sets accessibility standards for which province or country?
The Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) applies to which groups?
How is the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) enforced?
The Air Carrier Access Act (ACAA) applies to which of the following?
The 21st Century Communications and Video Accessibility Act (CVAA) applies to which of the following?
With all else being equal, which of the following would be the best way of creating a level 1 heading?
Which of the following is a reason why should you usually avoid using `role="application"`?
Which of the following is **NEVER** a valid landmark role?
Which of the following HTML tags is **ALWAYS** a landmark region?  Check all that apply.
Which of the following HTML tags is a landmark region?
Which HTML tag is equivalent to the `navigation` role?
Which HTML tag is equivalent to the `complementary` role?
What is one reason why is it important to use the appropriate ARIA roles when creating custom widgets, such as tabbed interfaces?
Which of the following is an ARIA widget role?
Which of the following is true regarding the `document` role?
Which of the following statements is **NOT** true regarding the `application` role?
Which of the following is a way to make paragraph text that appears inside of an `application` region readable by screen reader users?
Which of the following is **NOT** a reason why you should generally avoid making a lengthy paragraph focusable solely for the purpose of making it readable to screen readers?
Which of the following roles would automatically invoke Application mode in a screen reader?
Which of the following code examples would **NOT** result in content being ignored by a screen reader?
Which of the following code examples would **NOT** be announced as a heading by a screen reader?
What is the difference between the `note` role and the `complementary` role?
Should you ever assign an abstract role to an element in a web page?
What would **NOT** be an appropriate use for `aria-describedby`?
Will a live region with text in it on page load reliably be read automatically by screen readers?
What value should you set `aria-atomic` to for a region that dynamically displays chat messages?
If that same chat region only keeps the last 10 messages, removing a message every time it adds one, what should the value of `aria-relevant` be?
Which of the following live region types are set to `aria-live="assertive"` by default?
Which of the following live region types are set to `aria-live="polite"` by default?
Which of the following live region types are set to `aria-live="off"` by default?
Where is the best place to look for keyboard best practices for different kinds of widgets?
According to Deque University, which of the following is true regarding focus?
Which of the following is true regarding the order in which items receive focus?
Are there any exceptions to the rule that functionality available through a mouse must always also be made available through a keyboard?
How should the user navigate within a composite ARIA widget using the keyboard?
Which of the following is a way that you can activate a button using Dragon Naturally Speaking?
Which of the following is a way that you can activate a link using Dragon Naturally Speaking?
Which of the following is **NOT** a way to reliably notify blind users of dynamic changes to content on a page?
Which of the following is screen reading software likely to do automatically upon loading a new page?

A user has just entered the text string “registration deadline” in a site search field on a university website and hit the submit button.  What would be most likely to be the best title for the results page that loads?

A user has just entered the text string “registration deadline” in a site search field on a university website and hit the submit button.  What would be most likely to be the best title for the results page that loads?

Beside each item listed on the checkout page is a `select` element labelled, “Quantity,” that, when changed, dynamically updates the part of the page where the subtotal is displayed.  Which of the following is the best way to ensure blind users are notified of the change?

You want a screen reader to announce new content that has been added through an AJAX call by setting focus to it when the user clicks a button, but it is not being voiced.  According to Deque University, what might you have to do to fix this?
Which is usually the preferred strategy for dealing with lazy-loading content?
Which of the following is **NOT** a reason to programmatically associate a label with its associated form control?
Which of the following is a valid way to provide a programmatically-associated label for a form control?
Which of the following is the **BEST** way of labelling a form control?
Which of the following is **NOT** a reason for avoiding the use of the `placeholder` attribute as the label for a text field?
Why should you avoid using images of text as programmatically-associated labels for form controls?
Which of the following provides the **BEST** accessible name for a submit button for a search form that looks like a magnifying glass icon?
Which of the following techniques would **BEST** ensure that *all* users know which fields in a form are required?

A web-based email service allows users to delete messages by clicking an icon button whose `aria-label` attribute is set to "Delete Message".  The icon chosen looks like the null symbol, which is a circle with a diagonal slash through it (Ø). Which of the following is a reason why this might present a barrier?
Providing closed captions for a prerecorded video with an audio track is an example of which web accessibility principle?
Providing a text transcript for a an audio recording of an interview is an example of which web accessibility principle?
Providing audio descriptions for a prerecorded video with an audio track is an example of which web accessibility principle?
Using table markup to present tabular information is an example of which web accessibility principle?
Using label elements to associate text labels with form controls is an example of which web accessibility principles? **Check all that apply.**
Ensuring content is presented in a meaningful sequence when accessed with a screen reader is an example of which web accessibility principle?
Providing instructions to the user to click on a link to the right of another element on the page is a violation of which web accessibility principle?

The following example violates which accessibility principle?

Complete the assignments below. They will be graded as per the policy outlined in our syllabus.

Everything in **red** is due tomorrow:

- List of group members
- Project proposal
- Discussion forum assignment 4
- Quiz 4

And don't forget the game this Friday.  **Go Hokies!**
If increasing the size of the text 175% causes that text to be obscured by a picture on the page which accessibility principle would that violate?
Embedding informative text into a JPG image file is a violation of which web accessibility principle?
Providing a control near the beginning of the Web page that turns off sounds that play automatically is an example of which accessibility principle?
Ensuring keyboard control for all functionality is an example of which accessibility principle?
Ensuring that users are not trapped in content is an example of which web accessibility principle?
Providing a way for the user to turn the time limit off is an example of which web accessibility principle?
Allowing the content to be paused and restarted from where it was paused is an example of which accessibility principle?
Ensuring that nothing in a page flashes more than three times in any one second period is an example of which web accessibility principle?
Adding a link at the top of each page that goes directly to the main content area is an example of which web accessibility principle?
Providing a page title using the `title` element is an example of which web accessibility principle?
Ensuring focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability is an example of which accessibility principle?
Providing link text that describes the purpose of a link is an example of which accessibility principle?
Providing a main navigation menu, breadcrumb trail, and search functionality on every page is an example of which web accessibility principle?
If headings on a page fail to describe a topic or purpose, which web accessibility principle is violated?
Providing a visible focus indicator for all focusable elements on a page is an example of which web accessibility principle?
Ensuring that the default human language of each Web page can be programmatically determined by using the `lang` attribute on the `html` element is an example of which web accessibility principle?

Ensuring that any changes to the human language of the page can be programmatically determined by using the `lang` attribute on any element where the language has *changed* is an example of which web accessibility principle?
If a new browser window opened when a link received focus, which web accessibility principle would be violated?
Opening a new browser window without warning when changing the selected option in a select list would be a violation of which web accessibility principle?
Presenting navigation links in a different relative order on different pages in a set of web pages would be a violation of which web accessibility principle?
If you used different icons on different pages that initiated the same action when pressed, this would be a violation of which web accessibility principle?
If you opened a new window as soon as a new page is loaded, which web accessibility principle would this violate?
Providing text descriptions to identify fields that were not completed is an example of which web accessibility principle?
Providing instructions when content requires input is an example of which web accessibility principle?
Alerting users to errors in their input is an example of which web accessibility principle?

For web pages that causes legal commitments, providing a mechanism for reviewing, confirming, and correcting information before finalizing the submission is an example of which web accessibility principle?
For a show/hide pattern, appropriately setting the `aria-expanded` state of a button that hides and reveals content would be an example of which web accessibility principle?

Carrie is paralyzed and uses a power chair, from which she accesses the internet through a tablet mounted to her chair's arm. She controls the tablet using a sip-and-puff device. Which of the following would be **LEAST** likely to present a barrier for her:
Who would be **LEAST** likely to benefit when pages use the autocomplete attribute on form fields?

When a user hovers over a button (but not when it receives keyboard focus), text appears beside it.  When the mouse cursor leaves the button, the text's `display` property is set to `none`, making it disappear.  Which users would be **LEAST** likely to experience a barrier from this pattern?
Which of the following is **NOT** considered a "status message" according to WCAG 2.1?

If a keyboard shortcut is implemented in content using only letter (including upper- and lower-case letters), punctuation, number, or symbol characters, how can you ensure accessibility requirements are met for such shortcuts? 

**Select all that apply.**

` tag.  Which of the following should you do to ensure these are optimally accessible?
